items.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child

children.content: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

gallery.altText: Barry Cable walks up the stairs to court followed by a media pack.
Barry Cable seen walking down stairs with a TV camera in the foreground.
Shane Cable speaks on courthouse steps as his father Barry watches on.

thumbnail.altText: Barry Cable walks up the stairs to court followed by a media pack.
Barry Cable seen walking down stairs with a TV camera in the foreground.
Shane Cable speaks on courthouse steps as his father Barry watches on.

items.title: Father calls for accountability over 21-month-old son's death

children.content: The father of Sandipan Dhar, who died after not receiving a blood test at Joondalup Health Campus, says there is still a lack of accountability over his son's death.

image.altText: A boy dressed in winter clothes sitting on a black couch

items.title: Tow truck driver weeps after sentencing for fatal crash

children.content: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

image.altText: Rpss Gamage walks out of the Bunbury Courthouse after the decision.

items.title: Australia's biggest AI factory planned for remote WA

children.content: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is set to be built south of Broome in WA's Kimberley at a cost of billons of dollars.

items.title: Push to tax gas companies 'ripping up our oceans' as Gorgon plan approved

children.content: Natural gas taxes are back in the fray after the expansion of Chevron's Gorgon project off WA's north-west coast received environmental approval amid a Senate inquiry into how resources are taxed.

image.altText: A drone image of Chevron's Gorgon project on Barrow Island.

items.title: This country music singer's tour costs have got her rethinking her career

children.content: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair stands with a guitar strapped to her body in a home.

items.title: Prospector couldn't believe his luck when he found rare australite

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

gallery.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers
Older man in red shirt holds black tektite while smiling

thumbnail.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers
Older man in red shirt holds black tektite while smiling

items.title: Schools deemed safe after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire

children.content: Parents are assured they can safely send their children back to school in Perth's south-east, where a HAZMAT warning remains in place following a huge lithium-ion battery fire in Maddington.

gallery.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

thumbnail.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: The government is looking for cuts and thinks it has found its target

children.content: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a dark suit jacket, white shirt and red tie. He has a serious facial expression.

items.title: Automation is growing in Australia's mining industry — but what are the costs?

children.content: At Australia's largest gold mine, drivers are more likely to be found in a control room than a cabin, but researchers and unions say jobs and safety remain significant concerns.

image.altText: Newmont's gold mine near Boddington

items.title: 'This is not the way I'm going to go': Diver lost at sea details rescue

children.content: Stu Fillman was rescued alongside his dive instructor after being carried away from a dive company boat in rough swell.

image.altText: Mr Fillman has been left with lacerations to his neck.

items.title: The neighbourhoods where ADHD rates are skyrocketing

children.content: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

image.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

items.title: Dockers make light work of Eagles with 56-point Western Derby thumping

children.content: Fremantle maintains their Western Derby dominance with a big win over West Coast, as Jye Amiss kicks a career-high five goals to help the Dockers hold onto second spot on the AFL ladder.

gallery.altText: Dockers player Jye Amiss celebrates by high-fiving a teammate as two other Fremantle players stand behind.

thumbnail.altText: Dockers player Jye Amiss celebrates by high-fiving a teammate as two other Fremantle players stand behind.

items.title: Red-hot Perth ace Green blazes to third LA Championship triumph

children.content: Australian golf ace Hannah Green wins her fourth title of the season, taking out the LPGA Tour's LA Championship for a third time in four years.

image.altText: An Australian golfer smiles as she stands on a green holding a large trophy after winning an LPGA tournament.

items.title: Thrills and spills: How ex-NRL star Lomax fared in first Western Force start

children.content: Even if Zac Lomax's starting debut for the Western Force had been disastrous, it would still deserve to be considered a success.

image.altText: Zac Lomax celebrates scoring a try for the Western Force.

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of defaming actor with sexual harassment claim

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson is being sued in the Federal Court for defamation by one of the lead actor's in her directorial debut film, The Deb.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson arrives at the Federal Court of Australia

items.title: No police report filed over dramatic footage of boy being dragged by bus

children.content: Authorities are investigating how a 12-year-old became trapped in a bus door before being dragged hundreds of metres down a suburban street in Melbourne.

image.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

gallery.altText: A young couple standing in a kitchen
A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

thumbnail.altText: A young couple standing in a kitchen
A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

avatar.alt: A profile shot of ABC News journalist Lily Kristanto posing for a photo sitting as a desk with her hands clasped.

items.title: Kimberley diphtheria rates more than triple in a month after 50-year eradication

children.content: An outbreak of the long-dormant, highly contagious bacterial infection diphtheria in Western Australia's north is raising alarm among government officials and experts.

image.altText: A student receives a measles vaccine injection at the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL).

items.title: Grieving mother of Belgian backpacker warns hikers not to trust phone maps

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

image.altText: Ariane Mathieu, Celine Cremer's mother sits in a home space wearing a red sweater, a serious facial expression

items.title: Are you ever allowed to trim a council tree if it's near your property?

children.content: Expert advice on how to handle council trees causing problems on private property.

image.altText: A person chainsawing a tree branch

items.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network

children.content: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

image.altText: An older man with balding white hair holds up a rain gauge.

items.title: Aurora chasers hit the road as WA activity slows

children.content: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

gallery.altText: Red light from the aurora shimmers off the Molloy Island canal.

thumbnail.altText: Red light from the aurora shimmers off the Molloy Island canal.

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

image.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: Al Kuwait ships cattle out of Darwin after weeks anchored near Fremantle

children.content: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

image.altText: MV Al Kuwait at the Darwin Port

items.title: Two-thirds of Australians leave private information open to cybercriminals

children.content: New research suggests Australians are leaving themselves vulnerable to cybercrime through their public social media accounts.

image.altText: A pair of hands typing on a laptop

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: The mystery of an outback 'catch and kill' squad during WWII has been solved

children.content: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

gallery.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

thumbnail.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.
